perturbation.original_text: 6.01 Ownership of Program Results. ExxonMobil will solely own Program Information, Program Patents, and copyrightable works resulting from the Program (collectively, "Program Results"), irrespective of whether the Program Results are conceived, created, developed or acquired by employees or other representatives of FCE, ExxonMobil, or both. FCE will assign, and hereby assigns, to ExxonMobil ownership of Program Results.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.01 Ownership of Program Results. ExxonMobil will solely own Program Information, Program Patents, and copyrightable works resulting from the Program (collectively, "Project Outcomes"), irrespective of whether the Program Results are conceived, created, developed or acquired by employees or other representatives of FCE, ExxonMobil, or both. FCE will assign, and hereby assigns, to ExxonMobil ownership of Program Results.
perturbation.explanation: Here the term "Program Results" is changed to "Project Outcomes" in the definition within Section 6.01 while the last sentence still refers to "Program Results." This introduces a contradiction as it's unclear whether 'Project Outcomes' and 'Program Results' are interchangeable, creating uncertainty regarding the ownership of project deliverables. The use of 'Project Outcomes' in the initial definition implies a distinction from 'Program Results', but the continuation to use 'Program Results' in the assignation clause creates ambiguity on the proper term to refer to. This is particularly important as "Program Results" is specifically defined in the definitions, while 'Project Outcomes' is not, further driving a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.01 Ownership of Program Results. ExxonMobil will solely own Program Information, Program Patents, and copyrightable works resulting from the Program (collectively, "Project Outcomes"), irrespective of whether the Program Results are conceived, created, developed or acquired by employees or other representatives of FCE, ExxonMobil, or both. FCE will assign, and hereby assigns, to ExxonMobil ownership of Program Results.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Program Results" with "Project Outcomes" in the initial definition within Section 6.01, while the subsequent sentence still refers to "Program Results." This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether "Project Outcomes" and "Program Results" are interchangeable. The use of 'Project Outcomes' in the initial definition implies a distinction from 'Program Results', but the continuation to use 'Program Results' in the assignation clause creates ambiguity. This ambiguity directly impacts the enforceability of the clause regarding the ownership of project deliverables as it is unclear if FCE is assigning "Project Outcomes" or "Program Results" to ExxonMobil, fulfilling the criteria for Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.01 Program Information Disclosure, Confidentiality and Use Restriction. FCE will promptly disclose to ExxonMobil, in written or other tangible form, any and all Program Information including any Program Inventions. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, FCE agrees to hold Program Information in confidence, and not to disclose or make it available to any third party without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty (20) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, FCE agrees to use and practice Program Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 7 (License to Program Results).
perturbation.changed_text: 4.01 Program Information Disclosure, Confidentiality and Use Restriction. FCE will promptly disclose to ExxonMobil, in written or other tangible form, any and all Program Information including any Program Inventions. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, FCE agrees to hold Project Data in confidence, and not to disclose or make it available to any third party without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty (20) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, FCE agrees to use and practice Program Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 7 (License to Program Results).
perturbation.explanation: In section 4.01, the term 'Program Information' is replaced with 'Project Data' concerning the confidentiality obligations. This creates an inconsistency because the section initially discusses 'Program Information' and 'Program Inventions', then switches to 'Project Data' for the confidentiality agreement, and then reverts to 'Program Information' regarding usage, therefore it is unclear whether FCE is obligated to keep Program Information confidential in it's totality or just Project Data which is an undefined term within the text. This is a significant contradiction since the scope of confidentiality is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.01 Program Information Disclosure, Confidentiality and Use Restriction. FCE will promptly disclose to ExxonMobil, in written or other tangible form, any and all Program Information including any Program Inventions. Except as otherwise permitted under this Agreement, FCE agrees to hold Project Data in confidence, and not to disclose or make it available to any third party without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, for a period commencing on the Effective Date and ending twenty (20) years thereafter. Without the express prior written consent of ExxonMobil, FCE agrees to use and practice Program Information only for the Program or as authorized in Article 7 (License to Program Results).
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Program Information' with 'Project Data' in the confidentiality clause of section 4.01, while the surrounding sentences still refer to 'Program Information'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding what information FCE is obligated to keep confidential. The original text in 4.01 uses Program Information, and the changed text replaces this with Project Data, then returns to Program Information later in the same section, leading to uncertainty and therefore a contradiction because 'Project Data' is undefined. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: 3.01 Steering Committee. Promptly after the Effective Date, the Parties will establish a committee that will oversee technical support and provide overall supervision and administrative guidance for the Program ("Steering Committee" or "SC"), further detailed as follows - (a) Composition. Each Party will appoint in writing one or more of its employees as SC members. Each Party will have the right to change its SC members at any time by giving written notice of such change to the other Party. (b) Meetings. Meetings of the SC will be in person or by phone at a location and time agreed to in advance by the SC members. (c) Other Attendees. In addition to the attendance of SC members, with prior written notice to the other Party's SC members, each Party may also bring to any SC meeting such technical and other advisors as it may deem appropriate, provided that such advisors are employees of a Party or its Affiliates and are under written confidentiality and use restrictions at least as strict as those imposed herein. Otherwise, a Party's additional invitees may attend a SC meeting only with the other Party's advance written approval. (d) Responsibilities. The responsibilities of the SC will include, but are not limited to: i. Project Endorsement and Monitoring. The SC will review and approve each Project Description and amendment thereto prior to execution by the Parties. (However, no Project Description or amendment thereto will be effective unless and until it is executed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.) The SC will periodically monitor the ongoing status of all Projects, and make adjustments to priorities within and between the Projects. ii. Dispute Resolution. Assist the Parties in resolving any disputes. (e) Votes. Each Party only gets one vote on the SC regardless of the number of SC members it appoints. Except as otherwise stated in this Agreement, all decisions by the SC will be by unanimous agreement. In the absence of unanimity, ExxonMobil's SC representatives will have final decision making authority with respect to only the following decisions required by the SC: whether and where to seek patent protection and whether to maintain patent assets, subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6.04 (Solicitation of Program Patents Discretionary). (f) Minutes. All decisions by the SC will be documented in agreed upon minutes distributed to SC members after the meeting. (g) No Amendment Rights. The SC may recommend but has no authority to amend the terms and conditions of this Agreement. (h) Costs. ExxonMobil will bear its own costs associated with participating in the SC. FCE's costs and expenses associated with its participation in the SC are included in each Project's budget as Direct Costs.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.01 Steering Committee. Promptly after the Effective Date, the Parties will establish a committee that will oversee technical support and provide overall supervision and administrative guidance for the Program ("Steering Body" or "SB"), further detailed as follows - (a) Composition. Each Party will appoint in writing one or more of its employees as SC members. Each Party will have the right to change its SC members at any time by giving written notice of such change to the other Party. (b) Meetings. Meetings of the SB will be in person or by phone at a location and time agreed to in advance by the SC members. (c) Other Attendees. In addition to the attendance of SC members, with prior written notice to the other Party's SC members, each Party may also bring to any SB meeting such technical and other advisors as it may deem appropriate, provided that such advisors are employees of a Party or its Affiliates and are under written confidentiality and use restrictions at least as strict as those imposed herein. Otherwise, a Party's additional invitees may attend a SB meeting only with the other Party's advance written approval. (d) Responsibilities. The responsibilities of the SB will include, but are not limited to: i. Project Endorsement and Monitoring. The SB will review and approve each Project Description and amendment thereto prior to execution by the Parties. (However, no Project Description or amendment thereto will be effective unless and until it is executed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.) The SB will periodically monitor the ongoing status of all Projects, and make adjustments to priorities within and between the Projects. ii. Dispute Resolution. Assist the Parties in resolving any disputes. (e) Votes. Each Party only gets one vote on the SB regardless of the number of SC members it appoints. Except as otherwise stated in this Agreement, all decisions by the SB will be by unanimous agreement. In the absence of unanimity, ExxonMobil's SB representatives will have final decision making authority with respect to only the following decisions required by the SB: whether and where to seek patent protection and whether to maintain patent assets, subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6.04 (Solicitation of Program Patents Discretionary). (f) Minutes. All decisions by the SB will be documented in agreed upon minutes distributed to SC members after the meeting. (g) No Amendment Rights. The SB may recommend but has no authority to amend the terms and conditions of this Agreement. (h) Costs. ExxonMobil will bear its own costs associated with participating in the SB. FCE's costs and expenses associated with its participation in the SB are included in each Project's budget as Direct Costs.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Steering Committee" is changed to "Steering Body" initially, however, the section fails to correctly use the updated term and the shorthand acronym is not updated creating a contradiction. The composition subsection still refers to "SC members". Then the meetings and other attendees sections use both the new and old terms. The responsibilities are assigned to the SB, and the voting rights are determined with the SB in mind. The term SC creeps back into effect when referring to minutes distribution. In effect, this creates a mix of responsibilities, votes and composition challenges that don't clearly define which is which and in effect who has the final decision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.01 Steering Committee. Promptly after the Effective Date, the Parties will establish a committee that will oversee technical support and provide overall supervision and administrative guidance for the Program ("Steering Body" or "SB"), further detailed as follows - (a) Composition. Each Party will appoint in writing one or more of its employees as SC members. Each Party will have the right to change its SC members at any time by giving written notice of such change to the other Party. (b) Meetings. Meetings of the SB will be in person or by phone at a location and time agreed to in advance by the SC members. (c) Other Attendees. In addition to the attendance of SC members, with prior written notice to the other Party's SC members, each Party may also bring to any SB meeting such technical and other advisors as it may deem appropriate, provided that such advisors are employees of a Party or its Affiliates and are under written confidentiality and use restrictions at least as strict as those imposed herein. Otherwise, a Party's additional invitees may attend a SB meeting only with the other Party's advance written approval. (d) Responsibilities. The responsibilities of the SB will include, but are not limited to: i. Project Endorsement and Monitoring. The SB will review and approve each Project Description and amendment thereto prior to execution by the Parties. (However, no Project Description or amendment thereto will be effective unless and until it is executed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.) The SB will periodically monitor the ongoing status of all Projects, and make adjustments to priorities within and between the Projects. ii. Dispute Resolution. Assist the Parties in resolving any disputes. (e) Votes. Each Party only gets one vote on the SB regardless of the number of SC members it appoints. Except as otherwise stated in this Agreement, all decisions by the SB will be by unanimous agreement. In the absence of unanimity, ExxonMobil's SB representatives will have final decision making authority with respect to only the following decisions required by the SB: whether and where to seek patent protection and whether to maintain patent assets, subject to the provisions of Paragraph 6.04 (Solicitation of Program Patents Discretionary). (f) Minutes. All decisions by the SB will be documented in agreed upon minutes distributed to SC members after the meeting. (g) No Amendment Rights. The SB may recommend but has no authority to amend the terms and conditions of this Agreement. (h) Costs. ExxonMobil will bear its own costs associated with participating in the SB. FCE's costs and expenses associated with its participation in the SB are included in each Project's budget as Direct Costs.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Steering Committee" to "Steering Body" introduces misaligned terminology, which creates a contradiction within the document. The changed text inconsistently uses both "Steering Body" (SB) and "Steering Committee" (SC) without properly updating all references. For instance, subsection (a) refers to 'SC members,' while the main heading introduces 'Steering Body' (SB). Further, subsection (f) mentions 'SC members after the meeting' despite the initial intention to rename the committee. This inconsistent terminology, as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction,' creates a conflict because the roles, responsibilities, votes, and composition of the committee are not clearly defined, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The use of 'SC members' in some sections and 'SB' in others generates an ambiguity about which body holds authority and whose decisions are documented.
